rulings.Case Title: FIA CARD SERVICES N.A. VS. JESSE MANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; [Proposed] Order; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-03-2026. Line 1. DEFENDANT JESSE MANN'S Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment. The unopposed motion to vacate renewal of judgment is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: PERRY ZHANG ET AL VS. SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-03-2026. Line 4. PLAINTIFFS PERRY ZHANG, and YAN DUAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to amend is granted. Plaintiffs shall file the proposed First Amended Complaint attached as Exhibit A within one week. Here, Plaintiffs seek to amend to add punitive damages allegations. They allege that the corporate defendant had sufficient information of prior incidents involving nose-in accidents at storefronts to put the corporation on notice of the need to install safety features in places where pedestrians congregate. The court's summary judgment denial concluded there is a triable issue of fact here whether Sprouts had the requisite control to ensure these features. Plaintiffs allege that Sprouts' failure to act in response to its information demonstrates sufficient malice or oppression to justify punitive damages. The court grants leave to amend. Leave for a party to amend a pleading should be granted "in furtherance of justice." (Code Civ. Proc., section 473, subd. (a)(1).) "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Superior Court (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) Leave to amend is properly denied when the proposed amendment fails to state a valid cause of action and thus would be futile, or where the plaintiff has delayed in seeking the amendment and the delay has prejudiced the defendant. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739.) The court does not find futility or prejudice here. Sprouts identifies no prejudice other than a general statement of the need to take discovery and bring a summary judgment motion. Since amendments may be made on the eve of trial, it follows that there is no requirement that an amendment be made with time to make a summary judgment motion on the amendment. Sprouts identifies no particular discovery it needs prior to trial on a punitive damages claim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: CHI-DONG HWANG VS. ADIB KHOURI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set Two; And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-03-2026. Line 5. PLAINTIFF CHI-DONG HWANG'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set Two; And Request For Sanctions. The motion to compel further responses to requests for admissions, set two, is granted in part. The court orders defendants to pay partial sanctions of $1500 to plaintiffs within 30 days. This order uses the numbers of the RFAs to Adib Khouri. The order also applies to the RFAs to the trustees using the conversion chart at page two of Plaintiff's separate statement. The motion is denied as to RFA 29; this RFA is duplicative of RFA 22. For the following RFAs, the motion is denied because Khouri has provided a response: 31, 32, 35, 39, 40. Plaintiff's remedy if plaintiff believes Khouri should have admitted the matter is to seek the cost of proof at trial pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 2033.420. The motion is granted as to the following RFAs, for which Khouri's objections lack merit: 30, 36, 37, 38, 41, 42. Where further responses are ordered, Khouri and the trustees shall respond to these RFAs within two weeks. Plaintiff seeks sanctions but does not provide a requested lodestar amount. The court concludes that sanctions are appropriate and sets the amount at $1500 as a reasonable sanction for defendants' assertion of nonmeritorious objections and defendants' failure to respond to plaintiff's meet and confer regarding the issues on this motion. These aspects of defendants' position lacked substantial justification. Defendants are jointly and severally responsible for the sanction. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: DANIEL HERNANDEZ ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc. To Strike Or Tax Costs; Declaration Of Brandon R. Bell
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-03-2026. Line 7. DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'S Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs. Defendant's motion to strike or tax costs is granted as to the $150 jury fees deposited and is otherwise denied. "Code of Civil Procedure section 1033 enumerates allowable costs and costs which are not allowable, and restricts allowable costs to those reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation." (Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 129.) "To recover a cost, it must be reasonably necessary to the litigation and reasonable in amount." (Thon v. Thompson (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 1546, 1548.) Generally, if a cost item appears proper on its face, the burden is on the challenging party to prove it is not properly chargeable or is unreasonable. (Nelson, supra, 72 Cal.App.4th, at p. 131.) But "if the items are properly objected to, they are put in issue and the burden of proof is on the party claiming them as costs." (Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774.) Ultimately, whether a cost item was reasonably necessary is a question of fact, but the court has no authority to award costs that are not authorized by statute. (Id.) Jury fees are refundable unless jury trial is waived or continued with insufficient time to notify the jury that the trial will not proceed. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 631.3, subd. (a).) Here, trial was set for August 10, 2026. Defendant's motion is otherwise denied because the costs appear proper on their face and defendant does not show they are not properly chargeable. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
